status: Finalised — training about to start from scratch.
motivation: V6-Extended delivered stable language (no collapse) and monotonic val mIoU through Stage 1, but RWTD dropped 4.6 mIoU when Stage 3 unfroze SAM LoRA, and the best V6 live-inference honest number (exactly-2) plateaued at 0.69. A parallel Zero-Shot SAM 3 experiment using a highly descriptive, object-aware prompt (no VLM, no projector, raw SAM text path) achieved 0.928 mIoU on RWTD. This proves the geometry-blocker was not SAM and not language drift — it was the V6 Bottleneck Projector (4096 -> 512 -> 256) aggressively compressing Qwen's semantic richness before it reached SAM. V7 discards the bottleneck, widens the projection into SAM's native text-encoder space (1024-d), reuses SAM's own pretrained 1024 -> 256 resizer (frozen) for the final step, switches the LM-loss schedule from cosine to a sharp exponential cliff, and freezes SAM permanently.

architecture.seg_token: <|seg|> appended after each TEXTURE line (inherited from V5).
architecture.block_diagonal_mask: Decouples per-texture [SEG] representations (inherited from V5). V7 extends the filter to also skip any <|seg|> tokens rendered as literal text inside the user prompt template.
architecture.v7_new_1_bridge_projector: Replaces V6 bottleneck. Linear(4096, 1024) + LayerNorm(1024) + GELU + Dropout(0.4). Outputs into SAM3's native text-encoder width. Dropout(0.4) is load-bearing: the wider projection has 4.20M params (vs V6 bottleneck's 2.23M), so without heavy dropout the Directional Drift regression from V4 would reappear.
architecture.v7_new_2_frozen_resizer: SAM3's own backbone.language_backbone.resizer (Linear 1024 -> 256). Reused as-is, never trained. Carries SAM3's pretrained 1024->256 semantic mapping, effectively borrowing its vocabulary knowledge for free.
architecture.v7_new_3_exponential_lm_weight: Weight(d) = 1 - exp(-alpha * d), alpha=2.0, where d = token distance to nearest assistant-region <|seg|>. Weight at d=0 (the [SEG] token itself) is forced to 0.0 -- Qwen has total freedom to warp it for geometry. Weight at d=1 -> 0.865; d=2 -> 0.982; d>=3 -> near 1.0 (full LM supervision). Replaces V6's gentle cosine decay (lambda_min=0.05 -> lambda_max=1.0 over the whole block) with a sharp cliff that isolates the [SEG] geometrically but applies full LM pressure everywhere else.
architecture.v7_new_4_sam3_permanently_frozen: SAM3 never unfreezes. No LoRA on SAM. Justified by the ZS SAM 3 breakthrough (0.928 RWTD): SAM natively understands object-aware descriptions; fine-tuning it only destroys this OOD generalisation (confirmed in V6 ep10 -> ep15 RWTD regression).
architecture.v7_new_5_universal_prompt: Training and inference both use a single object-aware prompt. 'prominent foreground object and its background, or contrasting materials' replaces V6's texture-only 'contrasting materials, surfaces, or textures'. The format template also shows '<|seg|>' per TEXTURE line, giving Qwen explicit instruction/output alignment. Prompt-template <|seg|> tokens are filtered out of all downstream seg-position lookups (training labels, block-diagonal mask, hidden-state extraction, regex fallback).
architecture.v7_new_6_two_stage_curriculum: Stage 1 (epochs 1-12): Bridge + mask head + dustbin train at base LR 1e-4; Qwen LoRA frozen; SAM frozen. Stage 2 (epochs 13-20): Qwen LoRA unfrozen at 1e-6; Bridge base LR decays 10x (1e-4 -> 1e-5). No Stage 3.

trainable_parameters.delta_vs_v6: +1,971,328 params (+31.5%). All delta is in Bridge (wider output into SAM text space). SAM3 LoRA removed entirely.

v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.projector: V6: Linear(4096,512) + LN + GELU + Dropout(0.15) + Linear(512,256), 2.23M params. V7: Linear(4096,1024) + LN + GELU + Dropout(0.4), 4.20M params + frozen Linear(1024,256) reused from SAM.
v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.lm_loss: V6: cosine lambda_min=0.05 to lambda_max=1.0 per texture block. V7: exponential 1-exp(-2*d), SEG itself at 0.0.
v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.curriculum: V6: 3 stages with SAM LoRA unfreezing at Stage 3. V7: 2 stages; SAM frozen forever.
v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.prompt: V6: texture-only, 'material or surface'. V7: object-aware, 'material or object', example allows 'prominent foreground object and its background'.
v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.format_template: V6: 'TEXTURE_N: Texture of <description>'. V7: 'TEXTURE_N: Texture of <description> <|seg|>'. V7 requires prompt-side <|seg|> filtering in 4 extraction sites.
v7_vs_v6_diff_matrix.sam_lora: V6: rank 32 orthogonal LoRA active at Stage 3. V7: removed.

motivating_evidence_from_v6_runs.interpretation: ZS SAM's 0.928 sets the true ceiling of the geometric engine. V6's best honest live mIoU at 0.69 is 0.24 below this ceiling. The gap is almost entirely the projector's semantic squash.

next_experiments.current_status: Training launches from scratch. All V6 checkpoints moved to checkpoints/archive_v6/.
next_experiments.expected_trajectory: Stage 1 (12 epochs) purely Bridge warmup with Qwen frozen, expected to push val mIoU past 0.70. Stage 2 (8 epochs) unfreezes Qwen LoRA at 1e-6 with Bridge decayed to 1e-5.

hypotheses_to_validate.h1: V7 Bridge + frozen resizer closes most of the V6-to-ZS gap on RWTD
hypotheses_to_validate.success_criterion: RWTD live exactly-2 mIoU >= 0.80 at end of training

hypotheses_to_validate.h2: Exponential LM cliff eliminates the 'tug-of-war' between DICE and LM
hypotheses_to_validate.success_criterion: train_mask_dice drops below 0.30 by end of Stage 2 (V6 plateaued at ~0.43)

hypotheses_to_validate.h3: Permanent SAM freeze eliminates the Stage 3 OOD regression
hypotheses_to_validate.success_criterion: RWTD live mIoU is monotonic or plateaus; no regression after epoch 15

hypotheses_to_validate.h4: 4x projection (4096->1024) + 0.4 dropout preserves OOD generalisation despite 2x parameter count
hypotheses_to_validate.success_criterion: RWTD-to-ADE20K_val mIoU ratio > 0.85 (V4's 10.5M projector hit 0.81; V6 bottleneck hit 0.88)
